What a change this is! Sin darkens the beauty of even the highest powers and the best gifts; but that gold, tested by fire, which Christ gives, will never be taken from us; its outward appearance may be dimmed, but its true value can never be altered. The terrible events of the siege and destruction of Jerusalem are described again. Seeing the sad results of sin in the church of the past, let us seriously consider what the same causes might rightly bring down on the church now. But, Lord, even though we have turned away from you in rebellion, please turn to us, and turn our hearts to you, so that we may fear your name. Come to us, bless us with awakening, converting, renewing, and confirming grace.

Nothing prepares a people more for ruin, nor fills the measure faster, than the sins of priests and prophets. The king himself cannot escape, because divine vengeance pursues him. Our anointed King alone is the life of our souls; we can safely live under his protection, and rejoice in Him even when surrounded by our enemies, for He is the true God and eternal life.

Here it is foretold that an end would be put to Zion’s troubles. Not the full amount of punishment deserved, but what God has decided to inflict. An end will be put to Edom's triumphs. All the troubles of the church and of the believer will soon be finished. And the doom of their enemies draws near. The Lord will reveal their sins, and they will lie down in eternal sorrow. Edom here represents all the enemies of the church. And the corruption and sin of Israel, which the prophet has shown to be universal, justifies the judgments of the Lord. It shows the need for that grace in Christ Jesus, which the sin and corruption of all people make so necessary.
